overview: Phases 1-12 (plus 7 quick phases) are complete. Phase 13 adds JSON schema infrastructure for planning files.

phases.name: Claude Code Capability Mapping - Research and document all available plugin primitives as an audit reference
phases.goal: Create a complete reference of Claude Code's plugin primitives -- skills, agents, hooks, frontmatter options, context behavior -- as the standard to audit against
successCriteria: A capability reference document exists covering all skill frontmatter fields, agent definition syntax, hooks, and context behavior options
Each capability is documented with when to use it, examples, and tradeoffs (skill vs agent vs hook)
The reference is accurate against the current Claude Code version (verified by testing at least 3 capabilities)

phases.name: Add phase steps tracking to progress skill
Add branch guard and team log to execute-phase
Make brainstorm create context for roadmap phases
Add compaction resilience to execute-phase
successCriteria: Execute-phase skill has PreCompact and SessionStart(compact) hooks in frontmatter that re-inject critical state after compaction
Execute-phase writes EXEC-PROGRESS.md during execution tracking wave/plan completion, team name, and teammate state
cst-help is updated with guidance on compaction resilience and CLAUDE_AUTOCOMPACT_PCT_OVERRIDE configuration
phases.type: Quick phase -- lightweight planning, no research/verification

phases.name: Add research detection to phase-feedback skill
successCriteria: Phase-feedback skill includes an LLM analysis step between feedback collection and planner spawning that determines if research is needed
When research is needed, the skill spawns the phase-researcher agent inline and passes RESEARCH.md to the planner
phases.type: Quick phase -- lightweight planning, no research/verification

phases.name: Add planning file sync detection to progress skill
successCriteria: `/progress` includes a "Sync Issues" section that detects phase directories not listed in ROADMAP.md and ROADMAP.md phases without matching directories
`/progress` reports when STATE.md references a phase that doesn't exist in ROADMAP.md or has no directory
phases.type: Quick phase -- lightweight planning, no research/verification

phases.name: Add per-project toggle to disable simplifier agent in execute-phase
successCriteria: A `simplifier` preference in STATE.md (or equivalent per-project config) controls whether the code-simplifier step runs during execution
Execute-phase respects the setting in both task mode and teams mode, skipping simplification when disabled
phases.type: Quick phase -- lightweight planning, no research/verification

phases.name: Skill Audit & Reclassification - Systematically review every skill and classify as skill, agent, or hybrid
phases.goal: Systematically review every skill in the marketplace against the capability reference, producing per-skill recommendations
successCriteria: Every skill across all 3 plugins has been audited with findings documented
Each skill has a classification: remain as skill, convert to agent, hybrid, or needs feature additions
Specific frontmatter/feature gaps are identified per skill (missing tool restrictions, wrong model, missing context fork, etc.)

phases.name: Apply Audit Recommendations - Implement reclassifications, add missing features, fix frontmatter gaps
phases.goal: Implement all reclassification decisions and feature fixes from the audit
successCriteria: Skills classified for conversion are converted to agents (or hybrid) with working implementations
All frontmatter gaps are fixed -- every skill uses correct tool restrictions, model selection, context behavior, and argument hints
No regression -- every skill/agent functions correctly after changes (verified by manual execution of key workflows)

phases.name: Harden Fragile Areas - Address tech debt, phase numbering, state coordination, and large file decomposition
Workflow Validation & Gap Closure - Dogfood updated marketplace on a real project, discover and fix remaining gaps
Hook-Based Telemetry Capture - Add passive telemetry to orchestrator skills via shared shell script and skill-scoped hooks
phases.goal: Add passive, zero-token-cost telemetry to orchestrator skills via a shared shell script called by skill-scoped hooks
successCriteria: A `telemetry.sh` script exists in `plugins/claude-super-team/scripts/` that captures timing, agent spawns, tool usage, outcomes, and token usage
Orchestrator skills (plan-phase, execute-phase, research-phase, brainstorm) declare hooks in YAML frontmatter that call `telemetry.sh` at skill start/end and key lifecycle points
Telemetry data accumulates in `.planning/.telemetry/` in a format validated by research (JSONL, SQLite, or CSV)
Hook script gracefully no-ops when `.planning/` or `.planning/.telemetry/` directories don't exist

phases.name: Efficiency Regression Detection - Create /metrics skill for resource reporting and threshold-based violation detection
Build Skill Efficiency
successCriteria: Execute-phase reads teams-available from gather script PREFERENCES section instead of checking inaccessible env var (bug fix)
/build's adaptive pipeline depth heuristic accounts for project complexity class and tech stack coverage, not just keyword matching
Verification is skippable via a `verification` preference in STATE.md (always
Plan-phase planner instructions produce more aggressive wave batching -- plans that can safely run in parallel are assigned to the same wave
phases.type: Quick phase -- lightweight planning, no research/verification

phases.name: Full Auto Mode - Create /build skill that autonomously chains all pipeline skills to go from idea to fully built and validated application
phases.goal: Create a `/build` skill that autonomously chains the entire planning pipeline -- from idea to fully built and validated application -- using all claude-super-team skills with no user intervention, surviving many context compactions and self-validating its output at each stage
successCriteria: A `/build` skill exists that accepts a project idea as input and autonomously orchestrates: `/new-project` -> `/brainstorm` (autonomous mode) -> `/create-roadmap` -> and for each phase: `/discuss-phase` -> `/research-phase` -> `/plan-phase` -> `/execute-phase`
The skill makes autonomous decisions at every AskUserQuestion checkpoint, using LLM reasoning to select the best option without user intervention

The skill maintains a durable `BUILD-STATE.md` file that tracks the exact pipeline position (which skill, which phase, which step), all decisions made, and all validation results -- enabling full recovery after any context compaction

After each phase execution, the skill runs self-validation: builds the project, runs tests (if any exist), checks for errors, and uses `/phase-feedback` autonomously to fix issues before proceeding to the next phase
At completion, the user has a working application that builds and passes its own tests, with all `.planning/` artifacts documenting the full journey from idea to delivery

phases.name: Script Consolidation & State Compaction - Centralize duplicated gather scripts and add state compaction
Skill Capability Enhancements - Enhance /cst-help with artifact explanation and /build with dynamic completion awareness
Drift Detection - Create /drift skill comparing codebase against planning artifacts
New-Project Discussion Mode & Build Auto-Extend - Add interactive app definition to /new-project and make /build work from any project state
JSON Schema Infrastructure for Planning Files
phases.goal: Add structured JSON representations alongside top-level `.planning/` MD files (PROJECT, ROADMAP, STATE, IDEAS) so gather scripts can use `jq` for reliable data extraction, while preserving MD files as human-readable versions. Give `/cst-help` the ability to migrate existing MD-only projects to dual MD+JSON format.
successCriteria: JSON schema definitions exist for PROJECT, ROADMAP, STATE, and IDEAS -- each `.planning/*.md` file has a corresponding `.planning/*.json` with structured, queryable data
A shared script (`json-sync.sh` or similar in `plugins/claude-super-team/scripts/`) can generate JSON from existing MD content and validate JSON structure
All `gather-data.sh` scripts that read top-level planning files use `jq` on JSON files for data extraction, with fallback to MD parsing when JSON files don't exist (backward compatibility)
All skills that create or modify top-level planning files (`/new-project`, `/create-roadmap`, `/brainstorm`) produce both MD and JSON output on every write
`/cst-help` includes a "migrate" action that generates JSON files from existing MD-only `.planning/` projects, making them queryable without re-running the full pipeline

sprints.description: Planning files have dual MD+JSON format; gather scripts use jq; existing projects can migrate via /cst-help
